evidence.q1: Sections numbered sequentially 1 through 15; no internal cross-references to fail resolution.
evidence.q2: 'Personal Data', 'Processing', and 'Personal Data Breach' defined once in §2 and used consistently throughout.
evidence.q3: Specific commercial deadlines used, e.g., 'within 24 hours' (§7), 'within 2 business days' (§4).
evidence.q4: Modern commercial language: 'entered into by and between', 'supplements the parties' master services agreement'.
evidence.q5: Example: 'Vendor will maintain TLS in transit and an annual access review, and may replace other controls…' – grammatically coherent, no contradiction.
evidence.q6: Consistent register: §1 'For personal data processed under this DPA…', §15 'If this DPA conflicts with the master services agreement…' – same authorial tone.
